This suspect was identified as Joseph Leedy, a 40-year-old homeless man. After crashing through the doors of the jail lobby, he got out of the vehicle and was completely nude, except for a woman's blouse. Although he did cause thousands of dollars worth of damage to the building, he was not able to breach the steel doors. And fortunately, no one was standing in the lobby at the time of the crash. In response to the incident, the Martin County Sheriff's office plans to install concrete bollards in front of the jail to prevent similar occurrences in the future. After Leedy was restrained and given multiple doses of ketamine to sedate him, he was taken to a nearby hospital and was still uncooperative with personnel. Due to his resistance to ketamine, authorities suspect Leedy was under the influence of a controlled substance. He was medically cleared at the hospital and then taken back to the same jail he attacked. He was charged with four counts of aggravated assault on a law enforcement officer, criminal mischief of over $1,000, and resisting without violence. He is presumed innocent unless proven guilty in a court of law.
